backlogItem: Bug-fix sprint #6 in the V0.27 chain. Per CIO 2026-05-12 directive 'using your ideas and Spools build the next sprint' -- combines Spool's 4 V0.27.6-validation findings (Drive 11 captured cleanly Pi-side but server-side analytics fields all NULL + US-323 backfill never executed + US-324 drive_statistics ship incomplete + drive_counter still stuck) PLUS Marcus's PM finding I-030 (startup_log writer regression on V0.27.6). Per chain-end-merge rule: V0.27.7 branches FROM sprint/sprint32-bugfixes-V0.27.6 (cumulative chain), NOT from main. Spool's MAP PID feature ask (B-074) explicitly deferred to V0.28+ feature sprint per V0.27.X-is-bug-fixes-only standing rule.

testBaseline.note: Post-V0.27.6 baseline. Sprint 32 added 12 tests via US-325 backoff suite plus other stories' tests; verify exact count at sprint start. PRE-EXISTING ruff drift in tests/pi/regression/test_powersource_module_identity.py (8 UP037/F401 findings, V0.24.1 file) -- ruff-version drift since baseline; not in any sprint scope.

sprintNotes: GOAL: Close 4 V0.27.6-validation bugs surfaced by Drive 11 IRL run (Mike 2026-05-12). Drive 11 captured cleanly Pi-side (10,839 realtime_data rows; 470 rows/min) but exposed server-side analytics gaps: (1) drive_summary server-side analytics fields all NULL for Drive 11 -- US-326, (2) V0.27.6 US-323 backfill script never executed (rows 11-15 still NULL) -- US-327, (3) V0.27.6 US-324 drive_statistics ship incomplete (Pi-side table missing) -- US-328 Option C hybrid, (4) startup_log prior_boot_clean field empty on V0.27.6 post-Drain-17 boot -- US-330. A 5th candidate (US-329 drive_counter server-side stale) was DEFERRED 2026-05-12 to the V0.28+ server-schema-normalization epic (B-076) per BL-016 -- the CIO directive is 'drop the server-side drive_counter table', not 'compute it', and the table has zero server-side consumers; re-spec'ing a P3/no-consumer story to thread the Pi wire-protocol inside a patch sprint is disproportionate.

EXECUTION ORDER (parallel): TIER 1 (P1) -- US-326 + US-327 + US-328 (sync + backfill + drive_statistics Pi-table Option C). TIER 2 (P2) -- US-330 (startup_log regression). (US-329 removed -- deferred to B-076 / V0.28.)

THEME: 'Server analytics pipeline actually wired end-to-end.' V0.27 chain has shipped 6 prior bug-fix sprints with strong Pi-side capture, sync delta-table UPDATE, and Ollama decoupling. Drive 11 is the FIRST clean car-coupled drive on the chain (post-B-063 fuse-box install). It proved Pi-side works but exposed the server-side ANALYTICS tier as broken at every layer. V0.27.7 fixes the remaining server-side gaps so Drive 12+ produces the full chain: drive_summary INSERT/UPDATE -> sync delta -> drive_statistics per-parameter rows -> calibration CLI consumes them.

CONSTRAINTS (anti-blocker discipline + Sprint 28-32 lessons): (a) US-274 file-existence check ran during grooming. (b) US-282 commit-vs-claim verifier active. (c) feedback_pm_verify_diagnostic_premises.md: PM verified each premise empirically vs current code. (d) feedback_pm_verify_table_names_against_code.md: PM verified table names against Pi + server schema. (e) feedback_pm_run_pre_flight_during_grooming.md: PM ran pre-flight for all 5 stories. (f) feedback_pm_main_merges_at_chain_end_only.md: V0.27.7 stays on sprint branch; V0.27 chain doesn't merge until validated. (g) feedback_pm_patch_version_bug_fix_sprint_pattern.md: V0.27.X = bug-fixes only; MAP PID (B-074) defers to V0.28+. (h) Spool 2026-05-12 spec-discipline lesson reinforced: protocol-touching tuning specs MUST validate against empirical baselines before pinning numerics.

GROUNDING SOURCES: Spool 2026-05-12 Drive 11 validation note (Stories X/Y/Z/W) + Spool 2026-05-12 MAP PID note (deferred to B-074) + PM 2026-05-12 Drive 11 forensic pull (drive_summary row 15 analytics fields all NULL on server; startup_log empty prior_boot_clean field; drive_counter server still last_drive_id=3 vs Pi=11). Plus V0.27.6 chain-tip state (V0.27.6 stories US-320-325 all passes:true; awaiting full IRL validation).

POST-SPRINT-33 ACTION ITEMS (NOT in sprint.json per CIO no-human-tasks rule): (1) CIO completes Drive 12 to validate the V0.27.7 fixes (server-side drive_summary analytics writer + Approach-1 drive_statistics rows + Pi-side drive_statistics table exists + battery_health_log backfill ran). (2) Once V0.27 chain fully validated (Drive 12+ produces full pipeline output + Drain 18+ produces clean startup_log), CIO invokes /chain-validated (V0.27.5 US-318) to merge V0.27 chain to main and cut V0.28.0. (3) V0.28.0/V0.28+ candidates queued: B-074 (MAP PID), B-075 (drive_statistics Pi-side writer Approach 2), B-076 (server schema normalization epic -- owns the deferred US-329 fix + DROP TABLE drive_counter), B-077/B-078/B-079 (idle-chattiness + TZ bugs from the tester's 2026-05-12 db-review).

stories.title: drive_summary server-side analytics fields NULL for Drive 11 -- I-026
stories.intent: I-026: Drive 11 captured cleanly Pi-side. Pi-sync pushed drive_summary row to server (id=15, source_id=11 exists). BUT server-side analytics fields (Spec 3 fields 3-8: drive_id + start_time + end_time + duration_seconds + row_count + is_real) ALL NULL. Two hypotheses Ralph pre-flight identifies: (A) V0.27.4 US-315 sync UPDATE fix extended to battery_health_log but NOT to drive_summary delta path (INSERT-only B-065 pattern); (B) Server-side _ensureDriveSummary not firing for Drive 11 -- either trigger not invoked, NULL guard short-circuits, or V0.27.6 US-324 _writeDriveAnalytics wrapper regression. Pre-flight: server journalctl `_ensureDriveSummary|_writeDriveAnalytics` around Drive 11 sync window (2026-05-12 01:10-01:35Z). Present -> Hypothesis A. Absent -> Hypothesis B. Fix the identified path; add regression test.

stories.completionNotes: I-026 root cause: _ensureDriveSummary looked the existing Pi-sync row up by drive_id -- a mirror column the Pi-sync wire protocol never populates (it renames Pi PK drive_id -> id -> source_id) -- so the lookup always missed, the writer INSERTed a duplicate, and the UNIQUE(source_device, source_id) violation rolled the auto-analysis transaction back, leaving analytics fields 3-8 NULL (exactly Drive 11 server row 15). Hypothesis A (Pi-side sync UPDATE not extended to drive_summary) ruled out via code -- drive_summary IS in SYNC_UPDATE_TABLES_PK + server _upsertBatch does on_duplicate_key_update. Fix: lookup now matches source_id OR drive_id (ordered, .first()) and heals the drive_id mirror on UPDATE; src/pi/sync/client.py untouched. New regression test reproduces the IntegrityError pre-fix. Punted (PM): backfill of row 15 + historical drives 3/4/5 (won't self-heal -- deltas already consumed; I-026 AC allows punting); residual partial-row_count concern on multi-sweep syncs; journalctl confirmation of which Hypothesis-B sub-path fired for Drive 11.

filesActuallyTouched: src/server/services/analysis.py (UPDATE -- _ensureDriveSummary existing-row lookup now matches source_device AND (source_id==driveId OR drive_id==driveId), ordered by id ASC + .scalars().first(); UPDATE branch heals existing.drive_id = driveId; mod-history entry US-326)

tests/server/test_drive_summary_sync_update_propagation.py (NEW -- 3 tests / 2 classes; seeds a Pi-shape drive_summary + 110 realtime_data rows via runSyncUpsert then runs _ensureDriveSummary; RED pre-fix with sqlalchemy.exc.IntegrityError UNIQUE(source_device, source_id); covers idempotent re-run + analytics-first-then-Pi-sync lock-in)

grounding.value: Pre-flight done via code archaeology -- server journalctl on chi-srv-01 is a CIO/PM follow-up (same established pattern as US-323's deferred mysql baseline; Ralph does not run live infra). HYPOTHESIS A RULED OUT: drive_summary IS in src/pi/data/sync_log.SYNC_UPDATE_TABLES_PK = {'battery_health_log':'drain_event_id','drive_summary':'drive_id','dtc_log':'id'} AND src/server/api/sync._upsertBatch already does on_duplicate_key_update keyed on (source_device, source_id) for all tables -- both sides of the UPDATE-sync path are wired. HYPOTHESIS B CONFIRMED -- see next entry.
grounding.source: rg + Read of src/pi/sync/client.py + src/pi/data/sync_log.py + src/server/api/sync.py + src/server/services/analysis.py + src/pi/obdii/drive_summary.py

grounding.value: Root cause: _ensureDriveSummary looked the existing row up by `source_device==deviceId AND drive_id==driveId`. But the Pi-sync wire protocol renames the Pi PK drive_id -> id (src.pi.sync.client._renamePkToId fires because SYNC_UPDATE_TABLES_PK['drive_summary']=='drive_id' which != 'id') and runSyncUpsert maps the id field -> source_id, so a Pi-synced drive_summary row arrives server-side as source_id=N with the drive_id mirror column left NULL. The lookup-by-drive_id therefore never matched a Pi-sync row -> writer fell into the INSERT branch -> INSERT(source_id=N) tripped UNIQUE(source_device, source_id) -> IntegrityError -> the enqueueAutoAnalysisForSync transaction never committed -> analytics fields 3-8 stayed NULL/default. Exactly matches Drive 11 server row 15 (source_id=11, drive_id=NULL, start_time/end_time/duration_seconds NULL, row_count=0, is_real=0, but Pi-sync columns data_source='real'+ambient_temp_at_start_c=18+starting_battery_v=14.5 present).
grounding.source: code analysis cross-referenced against PM 2026-05-12 row-15 query (US-326 groundingRefs)

grounding.value: Fix (src/server/services/analysis.py:_ensureDriveSummary): existing-row lookup now `WHERE source_device==deviceId AND (source_id==driveId OR drive_id==driveId) ORDER BY id ASC` via .scalars().first() -- source_id is the real Pi-sync natural key; the OR drive_id keeps analytics-first INSERTed rows (which set both) findable; .first()+order keeps the pathological double-match deterministic without raising. The UPDATE branch additionally sets existing.drive_id = driveId to heal the NULL mirror (drive_statistics/calibration joins key on drive_id; a populated value also lets the next sync hit the drive_id index path). INSERT branch unchanged (already sets both source_id and drive_id). src/pi/sync/client.py NOT touched -- the scope's 'UPDATE if Hypothesis A confirmed' clause did not apply.

Verification: pytest tests/server/test_drive_summary_sync_update_propagation.py -> 2 FAIL pre-fix (sqlalchemy.exc.IntegrityError: UNIQUE constraint failed: drive_summary.source_device, drive_summary.source_id) then 3 PASS post-fix; pytest tests/pi/sync/ tests/server/ -m 'not slow' -> exit 0 (no regression); pytest tests/ -m 'not slow' --no-cov -> exit 0 (full fast suite GREEN); ruff check src/server/services/analysis.py src/pi/sync/client.py tests/server/test_drive_summary_sync_update_propagation.py -> All checks passed; python offices/pm/scripts/sprint_lint.py -> 0 errors.

STOP-CONDITION CARRY for PM (US-326 stopConditions + I-026 AC #3): (1) Server row 15 (Drive 11) + historical drive_summary rows for drives 3/4/5 will NOT self-heal -- the connection_log drive_end deltas are already consumed so _ensureDriveSummary won't re-fire for them; a one-off backfill script is needed (I-026 AC #3 explicitly allows punting this to a separate story). (2) Residual second-order concern out of US-326 scope: the auto-analysis trigger fires at drive_end-received time, when realtime_data for that drive may only be partially synced (per-table, batched at 500 rows/sweep), so the UPDATE could land a partial row_count/end_time on a multi-sweep sync -- a 're-trigger analytics after full sync' mechanism would be a separate story. (3) journalctl confirmation of which Hypothesis-B sub-path actually fired for Drive 11 (out-of-order Pi-sync-arrives-first vs trigger-not-invoked) is still worth a CIO check against chi-srv-01; the fix is robust to both paths but the journal would confirm.

stories.title: US-323 backfill wired into deploy-server.sh idempotent -- I-027
stories.intent: I-027: V0.27.6 US-323 shipped scripts/backfill_server_battery_health_log_stranded.py to populate server rows 11-15 (stranded pre-V0.27.4 with end_timestamp=NULL). Script exists in repo but rows 11-15 on server STILL show NULL. Likely: Ralph shipped the script with --dry-run default and no auto-run mechanism. Spool + PM agree Path B (durable mechanism) over Path A (one-off manual run): wire script into deploy-server.sh with idempotent guard (only writes when stranded rows exist; no-op after first success). Plus document the runbook for future migrations.

stories.completionNotes: I-027 confirmed by code archaeology (the live `ssh chi-srv-01` pre-flight is a CIO/PM follow-up -- same established pattern as US-326's deferred journalctl and US-323's deferred mysql baseline; Ralph does not run live infra): the V0.27.6 US-323 backfill script scripts/backfill_server_battery_health_log_stranded.py is correct + idempotent (planBackfill skips already-populated server rows; every emitted UPDATE keeps `AND end_timestamp IS NULL`; --execute requires a prior --dry-run sentinel) -- it was simply never auto-invoked (I-027 hypothesis (c)): deploy-server.sh had no step calling it and no systemd timer references it. Path B fix: new deploy-server.sh Step 4.6 + a new `--count-stranded` CLI mode on the script. `--count-stranded` is the cheap server-only pre-check (loadAddresses + loadServerCreds + scanServerRows -> countStrandedServerRows; no Pi SSH, no mutation, no sentinel) -- prints the number of configured drain_event_ids (default 11-15) still NULL server-side and exits 0 (rc 2 if the server is unreachable). Step 4.6 reads it: 0 -> no-op (idempotent re-deploys); >0 -> `--dry-run` then `--execute` (mysqldump backup, then the UPDATE batch in one transaction). Runs LOCALLY (the script SSHes to both server + Pi itself, same as Step 4.5 migrations); best-effort -- a Pi-unreachable / preflight failure logs a WARN and the deploy continues (not a `set -e` blocker; next deploy retries). Sentinel goes to ${TMPDIR:-/tmp} so an interrupted deploy never leaves a stray dotfile in the working tree. New regression test tests/server/test_backfill_stranded_idempotent.py (13 tests / 3 classes) RED pre-fix (11 fail: --count-stranded not a recognised arg -> argparse SystemExit; countStrandedServerRows AttributeError) -> 13 GREEN post-fix. Punted (PM): (1) live `ssh chi-srv-01` confirmation that the script exists at the deployed path + `--dry-run` would update 5 rows (acceptance #1 -- code-archaeology done; live confirmation is a CIO step). (2) real-world validation gate (post-V0.27.7 deploy -> server rows 11-15 populated, rerun no-op) is validation.bigDefinitionOfDone clause 2. (3) `.gitignore` does not list `.us323-dry-run-ok` / `.us240-dry-run-ok` -- Step 4.6 sidesteps via --sentinel-dir but a manual --dry-run in the repo root still drops the dotfile; minor housekeeping, out of scope per scope-fence.

filesActuallyTouched: scripts/backfill_server_battery_health_log_stranded.py (UPDATE -- new `--count-stranded` mode in the mutually-exclusive mode group + countStrandedServerRows(serverRows: Sequence[ServerDrainRow]) -> int pure helper (added to __all__); main() handles --count-stranded via loadAddresses + loadServerCreds + scanServerRows -> countStrandedServerRows, prints the count, returns 0 (returns 2 on BackfillError / _us209.MigrationError); module docstring run-examples + Purpose/Description block + mod-history entry US-327)

deploy/deploy-server.sh (UPDATE -- new Step 4.6 between Step 4.5 migrations and Step 4.7 systemd unit: `--count-stranded` server-only pre-check -> if >0 run `--dry-run` then `--execute` (both with `--sentinel-dir ${TMPDIR:-/tmp}`); best-effort (WARN, not `set -e` fatal); skipped on --restart; runs LOCALLY mirroring Step 4.5's apply_server_migrations.py pattern; header doc-block step list updated to cite Step 4.6 / US-327)

tests/server/test_backfill_stranded_idempotent.py (NEW -- 13 tests / 3 classes; StatefulServerFakeRunner models server-side battery_health_log state (an UPDATE batch flips the targeted rows NULL->populated, honours the WHERE id IN (...) scope) so a 'run twice' flow shows the second run is a no-op; SQL dispatch keys off the `input` payload because _runServerSql pipes SQL via stdin not argv; covers countStrandedServerRows (all-NULL / all-populated / mixed / empty / __all__ export), the --count-stranded CLI (prints count + exit 0, prints 0 when nothing stranded, server-only -- no sqlite3 call / no mutation / no sentinel, --drain-event-ids honoured, mutually-exclusive with --dry-run/--execute, rc 2 on server unreachable), and the deploy Step-4.6 flow run twice (first: 5 stranded -> 1 UPDATE batch -> all healed + sentinel cleared; second: count 0 -> skipped, updateBatchesSent stays 1) + --execute always needs a fresh --dry-run sentinel)

grounding.value: Pre-flight (code archaeology -- live `ssh chi-srv-01` is a CIO follow-up, same pattern as US-326 journalctl / US-323 mysql baseline): I-027 hypothesis (c) confirmed -- deploy/deploy-server.sh has NO step invoking scripts/backfill_server_battery_health_log_stranded.py and no systemd timer references it; the script itself is correct + idempotent (planBackfill skips already-populated server rows; renderUpdateSql keeps `AND end_timestamp IS NULL` on every UPDATE; --execute is gated on a prior --dry-run sentinel). Hypotheses (a) [--dry-run default, no execute] and (b) [script bug] ruled out -- the script works as designed; the gap is purely 'nothing auto-runs it'.
grounding.source: rg + Read of deploy/deploy-server.sh + scripts/backfill_server_battery_health_log_stranded.py + scripts/apply_server_migrations.py + offices/pm/issues/I-027

grounding.value: Fix: (1) scripts/backfill_server_battery_health_log_stranded.py gains `--count-stranded` (server-only -- loadAddresses + loadServerCreds + scanServerRows; no Pi SSH, no mutation, no sentinel; prints int, exit 0; rc 2 if server unreachable) + countStrandedServerRows(serverRows) helper. (2) deploy/deploy-server.sh Step 4.6 (after Step 4.5 because the end_timestamp/end_soc/runtime_seconds columns come from those migrations): STRANDED_COUNT=$(... --count-stranded ... || echo ERR); ERR -> WARN+skip; >0 -> `--dry-run && --execute` (both --sentinel-dir ${TMPDIR:-/tmp}); 0 -> no-op echo. Wrapped so a Pi-unreachable / preflight failure is a WARN, never a `set -e` deploy blocker. Mirrors Step 4.5's run-LOCALLY pattern (the backfill script SSHes to both hosts itself).

Idempotency: first deploy after V0.27.7 -> `--count-stranded` returns 5 -> `--dry-run` + `--execute` heal server rows 11-15 -> sentinel cleared. Every later deploy: `--count-stranded` returns 0 -> Step 4.6 is a pure no-op (no Pi SSH, no mysqldump, no UPDATE). Partial-then-retry is also safe: the plan-time skip of already-populated rows + the `AND end_timestamp IS NULL` guard on every UPDATE mean a re-apply touches zero already-healed rows; a stale --dry-run sentinel is overwritten by the next --dry-run.

Verification: pytest tests/server/test_backfill_stranded_idempotent.py -> 11 FAIL pre-fix (argparse SystemExit on --count-stranded; AttributeError on countStrandedServerRows) -> 13 PASS post-fix; pytest tests/scripts/test_backfill_server_battery_health_log_stranded.py -> 36 PASS (no regression on the existing US-323 script tests); pytest tests/ -m 'not slow' --no-cov -> exit 0 (full fast suite GREEN); ruff check scripts/backfill_server_battery_health_log_stranded.py tests/server/test_backfill_stranded_idempotent.py -> All checks passed; bash -n deploy/deploy-server.sh -> OK; python offices/pm/scripts/sprint_lint.py -> 0 errors.

CARRY for PM / CIO: (1) live `ssh chi-srv-01` pre-flight (acceptance #1: confirm the script exists at the deployed path; `--dry-run` reports it would update 5 rows 11/12/13/14/15) -- still a CIO step; the wiring is in place + tested synthetically. (2) Real-world validation gate (validation.bigDefinitionOfDone clause 2): post-V0.27.7 deploy on chi-srv-01 -> server rows 11-15 end_timestamp/end_soc/runtime_seconds populated matching Pi-side within tolerance; rerun deploy no-op. (3) `.gitignore` does not list `.us323-dry-run-ok` (or `.us240-dry-run-ok`); Step 4.6 routes the sentinel to ${TMPDIR:-/tmp} so a deploy never dirties the working tree, but a manual `--dry-run` from the repo root still drops the dotfile -- minor housekeeping, out of US-327 scope per scope-fence. (4) Carried forward unchanged from US-323: the server-side battery_health_log has NO *_vcell_v columns (BACKFILL_COLUMNS = end_timestamp/end_soc/runtime_seconds); the US-289 *_vcell_v rename was Pi-side only; still documented in the script docstring.

stories.title: drive_statistics Pi-side table migration (Option C hybrid) -- I-028
stories.intent: I-028 + BL-015 Option C (CIO 2026-05-12): Spool flagged Pi-side `Error: no such table: drive_statistics` + server-side 0 rows for Drive 11. Rex pre-flight (BL-015) established: (1) current architecture is Approach 1 (server-side computed by V0.27.6 US-324 _ensureDriveStatistics); (2) Pi-side never had the table -- it's a 'server-side addition' per drive_id.py:90; (3) US-326 this sprint very likely already cured the server-side '0 rows' symptom via the _writeDriveAnalytics transaction-rollback fix; (4) Spool's Story Z spec wants full Approach 2 (Pi computes + syncs) but constraint (h) V0.27.X=bug-fixes-only forbids that reversal -- deferred to B-075 for V0.28+. **CIO picked Option C (hybrid)**: ship a thin idempotent Pi-side CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS only (no writer, no sync change) so the Pi diagnostic query stops erroring + the table is ready for B-075. Server-side keeps the existing Approach 1 path (now fixed via US-326).

filesToTouch: src/pi/obdii/database_schema.py (UPDATE -- add idempotent CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS drive_statistics that columns-match the server-side schema; no writer wired)

tests/pi/obdii/test_drive_statistics_pi_table_migration.py (NEW -- regression test: (a) Pi-side table exists post-migration; (b) Pi-side query `SELECT COUNT(*) FROM drive_statistics` returns 0 not 'no such table'; (c) migration idempotent across re-runs on existing Pi DB)

tests/server/test_drive_statistics_writer_post_us326.py (NEW -- synthetic e2e: post-US-326 Pi-sync of a drive -> server-side _writeDriveAnalytics commits -> drive_statistics rows present server-side per Approach 1; would have FAILED pre-US-326)
filesToRead: offices/pm/blockers/BL-015.md (the architecture decision frame + Option C resolution)
offices/pm/issues/I-028-drive-statistics-ship-incomplete-pi-side-missing.md (background; note Option C narrows scope from the original 2-part frame)
src/pi/obdii/database_schema.py (existing CREATE-IF-NOT-EXISTS patterns; pick the matching ORM/raw SQL style)
src/server/db/models.py DriveStatistic ORM model (column shape to mirror Pi-side)
src/server/services/analysis.py _ensureDriveStatistics + _writeDriveAnalytics (verify Approach 1 path; verify US-326 transaction fix)
doNotTouch: src/server/services/analysis.py -- US-326's _ensureDriveSummary lookup fix this sprint is what cured the server-side gap; don't refactor or 'while-we're-here' it
src/server/analytics/basic.py:computeDriveStatistics -- the Approach 1 writer; do not retire or alter
src/pi/sync/client.py + src/pi/data/sync_log.py -- no Pi-side writer in Option C, no sync change, no IN_SCOPE_TABLES expansion. Approach 2 sync work is B-075 for V0.28+.
src/pi/obdii/drive_summary.py + any Pi-side compute logic -- no Pi-side writer in this story. Empty table is the deliverable.
realtime_data writer or schema

groundingRefs.value: PM 2026-05-12 verified: Pi-side sqlite query `SELECT * FROM drive_statistics LIMIT 1` -> `Error: no such table: drive_statistics`
Server-side query `SELECT COUNT(*) FROM drive_statistics WHERE drive_id=11` -> 0 rows (also 0 rows for all prior drives 1-10)
BL-015 resolution 2026-05-12: CIO picked Option C (hybrid) over Spool's full Approach 2 spec; Approach 2 deferred to B-075 for V0.28+ feature sprint per V0.27.X-is-bug-fixes-only standing rule

Rex pre-flight (BL-015): US-326 this sprint very likely already cured the server-side 0-rows symptom -- _writeDriveAnalytics transaction rolled back pre-fix due to _ensureDriveSummary IntegrityError; US-326 commit goes through -> Approach 1 _ensureDriveStatistics persists rows for Drive 12+

Real-world validation gate: Drive 12 post-V0.27.7 deploy -> Pi-side `SELECT COUNT(*) FROM drive_statistics` returns 0 not an error; server-side drive_statistics rows present for canonical PIDs (RPM/COOLANT_TEMP/IAT/MAF/TPS/LTFT/STFT/TIMING_ADVANCE/BAT_V) via existing Approach 1 path

acceptance: Pre-flight audit: rg `drive_statistics` src/pi/ tests/pi/ -- confirm no existing Pi-side writer or schema entry (BL-015 pre-flight conclusion); read src/server/db/models.py DriveStatistic ORM model to lift the column shape; pick the CREATE-IF-NOT-EXISTS style that matches sibling Pi-side tables in database_schema.py

Pi-side `drive_statistics` table exists post-migration (idempotent CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS in src/pi/obdii/database_schema.py); columns match server-side schema; migration runs idempotently on already-migrated Pi DBs
Pi-side query `SELECT COUNT(*) FROM drive_statistics` returns 0 (not 'no such table' error)

Synthetic post-US-326 e2e test asserts that a Pi-sync delivering a drive (drive_summary + realtime_data) produces drive_statistics rows server-side via existing Approach 1 (_writeDriveAnalytics path); would have FAILED pre-US-326 (transaction rollback)
No writer wired Pi-side (Option C explicit); no sync IN_SCOPE_TABLES change; no Approach 1 -> 2 reversal -- those are deferred to B-075 for V0.28+
invariants: Pi-side CREATE IF NOT EXISTS migration is idempotent; pre-existing Pi DBs upgrade without data loss + re-deploy is no-op
Pi-side table stays EMPTY (no writer in Option C; rows-populated-on-Pi is B-075 V0.28+ work)
Server-side Approach 1 path (V0.27.6 US-324 _ensureDriveStatistics + V0.27.7 US-326 transaction fix) stays the producer of drive_statistics rows; this story does NOT touch that path
Calibration CLI (scripts/report.py --calibrate) can JOIN drive_summary x drive_statistics without missing-table errors (Pi-side join is empty but exists; server-side join produces rows post-US-326)
stopConditions: If pre-flight reveals US-326's fix does NOT in fact resolve server-side drive_statistics row creation (e.g., a separate guard short-circuits _ensureDriveStatistics) -- STOP, document; PM evaluates a server-side trigger fix as a separate story
If Pi-side CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS would require dropping or migrating an existing Pi `drive_statistics` table (none expected per pre-flight, but if discovered) -- STOP, document; do not auto-drop
stories.completionNotes: BL-015 Option C (hybrid) shipped: a thin idempotent Pi-side `drive_statistics` table only -- no Pi-side writer, no sync change. Added SCHEMA_DRIVE_STATISTICS to src/pi/obdii/database_schema.py (raw-SQL CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS, matching the sibling SCHEMA_* style in that file) + the `('drive_statistics', SCHEMA_DRIVE_STATISTICS)` entry in ALL_SCHEMAS, so ObdDatabase.initialize() (already idempotent, runs every Pi boot) creates it on fresh DBs AND on existing pre-US-328 DBs (next-boot migration -- no separate ensureXxxTable helper needed). Column shape mirrors the server ORM src/server/db/models.py:DriveStatistic EXACTLY: id INTEGER PK AUTOINCREMENT, drive_id INTEGER NOT NULL, parameter_name TEXT NOT NULL, min_value/max_value/avg_value/std_dev/outlier_min/outlier_max REAL nullable, sample_count INTEGER nullable, computed_at DATETIME NOT NULL DEFAULT (TD-027 canonical strftime ISO-8601 UTC -- the Pi capture-table convention; the server's nullable+server_default is the analogous form). No FK constraint (the server model has none either). No data_source/sync columns (no Pi-side writer in Option C; the server's drive_statistics has no such column). PRE-FLIGHT confirmed BL-015's findings: no existing Pi-side drive_statistics writer or schema entry; server-side path = Approach 1 (_writeDriveAnalytics -> _ensureDriveSummary then _ensureDriveStatistics -> computeDriveStatistics reading synced realtime_data); US-326's _ensureDriveSummary lookup fix THIS sprint is what un-broke the server-side '0 rows for Drive 11' symptom (pre-fix the duplicate-key INSERT raised IntegrityError -> the auto-analysis transaction rolled back before _ensureDriveStatistics committed). Two new test files: tests/pi/obdii/test_drive_statistics_pi_table_migration.py (17 tests / 6 classes -- table created on fresh DB, column shape == server ORM set, id is PK, drive_id+parameter_name NOT NULL, aggregates nullable, `SELECT COUNT(*) FROM drive_statistics` returns 0 not 'no such table', table ships EMPTY (no writer), computed_at DEFAULT is canonical ISO-8601 UTC, initialize() twice is a no-op, pre-existing rows survive a re-init, a legacy DB missing the table picks it up on next boot without disturbing other tables, DDL constant uses CREATE IF NOT EXISTS + strftime + no data_source column, the pinned column set == DriveStatistic.__table__.columns when SQLAlchemy is importable; RED pre-fix -- table absent, SCHEMA_DRIVE_STATISTICS attr absent) + tests/server/test_drive_statistics_writer_post_us326.py (3 tests / 2 classes -- a Pi-synced drive (realtime_data with RPM/COOLANT_TEMP/SPEED interleaved + a Pi-shape drive_summary row with source_id set / drive_id mirror NULL) run through _writeDriveAnalytics produces one drive_statistics row per parameter keyed on the SERVER drive_summary.id with sample_count + min<=avg<=max populated; idempotent on re-run; the Option-C contract -- the Pi sent NO drive_statistics rows, the server computed them; would have FAILED pre-US-326 because _ensureDriveSummary raised IntegrityError before _ensureDriveStatistics ran -- discriminator verified by code analysis, not by reverting US-326 which is doNotTouch this story). Verification all green: pytest the two new files -> 20 PASS; pytest tests/ -m 'not slow' --no-cov -> exit 0 (full fast suite GREEN, baseline also exit 0); ruff check src/pi/obdii/database_schema.py + both new test files -> All checks passed; python offices/pm/scripts/sprint_lint.py -> 0 errors. doNotTouch honoured: src/server/services/analysis.py, src/server/analytics/basic.py, src/pi/sync/client.py, src/pi/data/sync_log.py, src/pi/obdii/drive_summary.py, realtime_data writer/schema -- all byte-frozen; only src/pi/obdii/database_schema.py + the 2 new test files touched. Punted (PM): (1) live `ssh chi-eclipse-01` confirmation that `SELECT * FROM drive_statistics` no longer errors on the deployed Pi (acceptance done by code archaeology + synthetic test; live confirmation is the validation.bigDefinitionOfDone clause-3 CIO step -- same deferred-live-infra pattern as US-326 journalctl / US-327 ssh / US-323 mysql). (2) Real-world gate: Drive 12 post-V0.27.7 deploy -> Pi `SELECT COUNT(*) FROM drive_statistics` returns 0 not an error AND server-side drive_statistics rows present for canonical PIDs via the Approach-1 path (bigDoD clause 3). (3) The journalctl confirmation of which Hypothesis-B sub-path fired for Drive 11 (carried from US-326) and the historical-drives backfill of drive_summary rows 3/4/5/15 + their drive_statistics (carried from US-326/US-327) are still open PM/CIO follow-ups; out of US-328 scope. (4) Full Approach 2 (Pi-side compute-at-drive-end writer + drive_statistics joins the Pi sync registry) is filed as B-075 for the V0.28.0 feature sprint per BL-015 -- the Pi-side table US-328 ships is the migration foundation B-075 builds on.

filesActuallyTouched: src/pi/obdii/database_schema.py (UPDATE -- new SCHEMA_DRIVE_STATISTICS raw-SQL constant: `CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS drive_statistics` mirroring the server ORM column shape (id PK AUTOINCREMENT / drive_id + parameter_name NOT NULL / min_value/max_value/avg_value/std_dev/outlier_min/outlier_max REAL / sample_count INTEGER / computed_at DATETIME NOT NULL DEFAULT canonical-strftime; no FK, no data_source/sync columns); added `('drive_statistics', SCHEMA_DRIVE_STATISTICS)` to ALL_SCHEMAS so ObdDatabase.initialize() creates it idempotently on fresh + existing Pi DBs; mod-history entry US-328 + a long block comment documenting Option C / B-075)

tests/pi/obdii/test_drive_statistics_pi_table_migration.py (NEW -- 17 tests / 6 classes; freshDb fixture via ObdDatabase(...).initialize(); RED pre-fix on table absence + missing SCHEMA_DRIVE_STATISTICS attr; covers table creation, column-shape == server ORM column set, PK, NOT NULL constraints, nullable aggregates, `SELECT COUNT(*)` == 0 (the acceptance criterion), empty-table/no-writer invariant, canonical computed_at DEFAULT, initialize()-twice no-op, row-survives-reinit, legacy-DB-missing-table picks-it-up-on-next-boot without disturbing drive_counter, DDL-string sanity (CREATE IF NOT EXISTS / strftime / no data_source), cross-check against src.server.db.models.DriveStatistic.__table__.columns guarded by pytest.importorskip('sqlalchemy'), and a NOT NULL rejection)

tests/server/test_drive_statistics_writer_post_us326.py (NEW -- 3 tests / 2 classes; in-memory SQLite server engine via Base.metadata.create_all; seeds a Pi-shape drive_summary (source_id set, drive_id mirror NULL) + interleaved RPM/COOLANT_TEMP/SPEED realtime_data via the real runSyncUpsert, then runs _writeDriveAnalytics and asserts one drive_statistics row per parameter keyed on the SERVER drive_summary.id with sample_count + min<=avg<=max populated, the drive_summary reconciled-in-place (US-326) not duplicated; idempotent-on-re-run; the Option-C contract that the rows are server-computed not Pi-synced; pre-US-326 this path produced ZERO drive_statistics rows because _ensureDriveSummary raised IntegrityError -- discriminator verified by code analysis since reverting US-326 is doNotTouch)

grounding.value: Pre-flight (code archaeology -- live `ssh chi-eclipse-01` is a CIO follow-up, same established pattern as US-326 journalctl / US-327 ssh / US-323 mysql): confirmed BL-015's findings -- (1) `rg drive_statistics src/pi/ tests/pi/` shows NO Pi-side writer and NO schema entry pre-US-328 (src/pi/obdii/drive_id.py:90 calls it a 'server-side addition'); not in any Pi sync set (sync_log.PK_COLUMN / DELTA_SYNC_TABLES / SNAPSHOT_TABLES / SYNC_UPDATE_TABLES_PK). (2) Server-side architecture = Approach 1: src/server/services/analysis.py _writeDriveAnalytics -> _ensureDriveSummary then _ensureDriveStatistics -> src/server/analytics/basic.py computeDriveStatistics (reads realtime_data in the drive's window keyed on source_device + start_time/end_time, upserts one drive_statistics row per distinct parameter_name; idempotent -- DELETEs prior rows then re-inserts; drive_statistics.drive_id keys the server-side DriveSummary.id). (3) Spool's '0 rows for Drive 11' symptom is the downstream consequence of the I-026 bug US-326 fixed THIS sprint: pre-fix _ensureDriveSummary's lookup-by-drive_id missed the Pi-sync row (drive_id mirror NULL), the writer INSERTed a duplicate, tripped UNIQUE(source_device, source_id) -> IntegrityError -> the auto-analysis transaction rolled back before _ensureDriveStatistics committed. Hypotheses 'Pi never had a writer' (true -- by design under Approach 1) + 'server didn't fire' (it fired but rolled back) both confirmed; US-326's lookup fix lets the transaction commit, so the Approach-1 writer now lands the rows for Drive 12+.
grounding.source: rg + Read of src/pi/obdii/database_schema.py + src/pi/obdii/database.py + src/pi/obdii/drive_id.py + src/pi/data/sync_log.py + src/server/db/models.py + src/server/services/analysis.py + src/server/analytics/basic.py + offices/pm/blockers/BL-015.md + offices/pm/issues/I-028

grounding.value: Fix (Option C / BL-015): src/pi/obdii/database_schema.py gains SCHEMA_DRIVE_STATISTICS = `CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS drive_statistics (id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT, drive_id INTEGER NOT NULL, parameter_name TEXT NOT NULL, min_value REAL, max_value REAL, avg_value REAL, std_dev REAL, outlier_min REAL, outlier_max REAL, sample_count INTEGER, computed_at DATETIME NOT NULL DEFAULT (strftime('%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ', 'now')))` -- columns match src/server/db/models.py:DriveStatistic exactly; no FK (server has none); no data_source/sync columns (no Pi-side writer in Option C). Added `('drive_statistics', SCHEMA_DRIVE_STATISTICS)` to ALL_SCHEMAS (appended after startup_log -- no FK so ordering is free). ObdDatabase.initialize() already iterates ALL_SCHEMAS with `cursor.execute(schema)` on every Pi boot and is idempotent, so this covers both fresh DBs and the next-boot migration of pre-US-328 production DBs; no separate ensureDriveStatisticsTable helper needed. NO Pi-side writer wired; NO sync change; NO IN_SCOPE_TABLES expansion -- those are B-075/V0.28. The Pi-side table ships EMPTY and that is the deliverable.

Idempotency / migration: a fresh `ObdDatabase(...).initialize()` creates drive_statistics; a second `initialize()` on the same DB is a no-op (CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS) and preserves any rows; a legacy DB that has the rest of the schema but not drive_statistics picks the table up on the next `initialize()` (= next Pi boot) without disturbing other tables (test seeds a drive_counter row at last_drive_id=11 and asserts it's intact after the migration). Calibration (scripts/report.py --calibrate) can JOIN drive_summary x drive_statistics without a missing-table error -- the Pi-side join is empty (no writer) but the table exists; the server-side join produces rows post-US-326.

Verification: pytest tests/pi/obdii/test_drive_statistics_pi_table_migration.py -> 17 PASS (RED pre-fix: 15 fail on table-absent + AttributeError on SCHEMA_DRIVE_STATISTICS, then GREEN); pytest tests/server/test_drive_statistics_writer_post_us326.py -> 3 PASS; pytest tests/ -m 'not slow' --no-cov -> exit 0 (full fast suite GREEN; the pre-change baseline run was also exit 0); ruff check src/pi/obdii/database_schema.py tests/pi/obdii/test_drive_statistics_pi_table_migration.py tests/server/test_drive_statistics_writer_post_us326.py -> All checks passed; python offices/pm/scripts/sprint_lint.py -> 0 errors (3 warnings, all 'feedback should be {...}; got []' for US-329/US-330 not-yet-done -- US-328's feedback now populated). bash -n / config validation not applicable (no shell or config files touched).

CARRY for PM / CIO: (1) live `ssh chi-eclipse-01` confirmation that `SELECT * FROM drive_statistics` no longer errors on the deployed Pi (acceptance #3 -- done by code archaeology + synthetic test here; live confirmation is the validation.bigDefinitionOfDone clause-3 CIO step). (2) Real-world gate (bigDoD clause 3): Drive 12 post-V0.27.7 deploy -> Pi `SELECT COUNT(*) FROM drive_statistics` returns 0 not an error AND server-side drive_statistics rows present for canonical PIDs (RPM/COOLANT_TEMP/IAT/MAF/TPS/LTFT/STFT/TIMING_ADVANCE/BAT_V) via the Approach-1 path (V0.27.6 US-324 + V0.27.7 US-326 transaction fix). (3) Carried forward from US-326/US-327 (out of US-328 scope): journalctl confirmation of which Hypothesis-B sub-path fired for Drive 11; one-off backfill of drive_summary rows 3/4/5/15 + their drive_statistics (won't self-heal -- deltas already consumed); the 're-trigger analytics after full sync' second-order concern for multi-sweep syncs. (4) Full Approach 2 (Pi-side compute-at-drive-end writer + drive_statistics in the Pi sync registry + server-side ingestion of synced rows / retire the server-side compute-on-sync writer) is filed as B-075 for the V0.28.0 feature sprint per BL-015 -- the empty Pi-side table US-328 ships is the migration foundation B-075 builds on; do NOT attempt the Approach-2 reversal in any further V0.27.X patch sprint.
grounding.source: US-328 acceptance + stopConditions + BL-015 + I-028 acceptance criteria

stories.title: startup_log prior_boot_clean regression on V0.27.6 -- I-030
stories.intent: I-030: Post-Drain-17 boot (V0.27.6) wrote a startup_log row with prior_boot_clean field EMPTY (not 1, not 0, blank). Earlier boots had prior_boot_clean=1. Regression from V0.27.4/5 behavior. Hypotheses Ralph pre-flight identifies: (A) V0.27.6 US-322's new orphan-cleanup.timer creates race with boot_reason.py writer; (B) V0.27.6 deploy changed Pi boot order; (C) Ladder shutdown sequence on V0.27.6 differs from V0.27.4 in a way that breaks graceful-detection regex. Pre-flight: capture journalctl --boot=-1 from chi-eclipse-01 (post-Drain-17 boot); compare graceful-shutdown log lines vs V0.27.4 reference. Fix identified path; regression test feeds V0.27.6-flavor journal slice -> writer produces prior_boot_clean=1.

filesToTouch: src/pi/diagnostics/boot_reason.py (UPDATE -- fix per identified hypothesis: race-guard, boot-order timing, or graceful-detection regex update)
tests/pi/diagnostics/test_boot_reason_v0276_graceful.py (NEW -- regression test: feed V0.27.6-flavor journal slice; assert prior_boot_clean=1; would FAIL pre-fix)
filesToRead: src/pi/diagnostics/boot_reason.py (V0.27.2 US-308 graceful detection + US-287 writer)
V0.27.4 reference: chi-eclipse-01 startup_log row from Drain 15 boot (populated prior_boot_clean=1); use journalctl --boot=-3 or earlier as comparison source
doNotTouch: US-308 graceful detection logic if Hypothesis A/B (race or boot order); only adjust timing/ordering
orphan-cleanup.timer schedule unless Hypothesis A confirmed AND fix requires unit reordering

groundingRefs.value: PM 2026-05-12 chi-eclipse-01 sqlite query: most recent startup_log row recorded_at=2026-05-12T00:37:02Z, prior_boot_clean=(empty), prior_last_entry_ts=(empty)
Earlier V0.27.4/5 boots populated prior_boot_clean=1 with prior_last_entry_ts; regression cliff at V0.27.6 deploy boundary
Real-world validation gate: Drain 18+ followed by graceful boot post-V0.27.7 deploy -> startup_log row has prior_boot_clean=1 + prior_last_entry_ts populated

acceptance: Pre-flight: capture journalctl --boot=-1 from chi-eclipse-01 (post-Drain-17 boot); compare graceful-shutdown log lines vs V0.27.4 Drain 15 reference; identify hypothesis (A/B/C)
Fix lands per identified hypothesis; document choice + rationale in feedback.grounding
Regression test tests/pi/diagnostics/test_boot_reason_v0276_graceful.py: V0.27.6-flavor journal slice -> writer produces prior_boot_clean=1; FAILS pre-fix
(Optional) Backfill empty row from Drain 17 boot using journalctl --boot=-1 evidence -- yes/no per PM evaluation
verification: pytest tests/pi/diagnostics/ -m 'not slow' -q (no regression on V0.27.2 US-308 + V0.27.4 graceful tests)
invariants: Real-world validation gate: Drain 18+ post-V0.27.7 produces populated prior_boot_clean field
V0.27.2 US-308 graceful detection invariant preserved (the regression doesn't expand to graceful=false on graceful boots)
boot_reason.py writer is idempotent + retries safe (no duplicate startup_log rows on race)
stopConditions: If pre-flight reveals NEITHER Hypothesis A/B/C matches -- STOP, document; PM evaluates whether scope expands or hypothesis discovery is the deliverable
If orphan-cleanup.timer (US-322) is the cause AND reordering breaks other invariants -- STOP, escalate; race-guard approach vs unit-ordering approach

filesActuallyTouched: src/pi/diagnostics/boot_reason.py (UPDATE -- (a) new constants LIST_BOOTS_RETRY_ATTEMPTS=3 + LIST_BOOTS_RETRY_SLEEP_SECONDS=2.0 (added to __all__); (b) new _readBootList(journalctlRunner, sleeper) helper (added to __all__) -- retries `journalctl --list-boots` on the None failure sentinel up to LIST_BOOTS_RETRY_ATTEMPTS with a LIST_BOOTS_RETRY_SLEEP_SECONDS backoff, INFO-logs a recovered attempt, WARNING-logs each retry + a final 'unavailable after N attempts' bail, returns the parsed BootListEntry list (a non-None-but-unparseable result is a real result -> NOT retried) or [] when every attempt fails; (c) detectBootReason gains a `sleeper: Callable[[float], None] = time.sleep` kwarg and now calls _readBootList(journalctlRunner, sleeper) instead of a single journalctlRunner(['--list-boots']) -- the listOutput-is-None early-return is folded into the existing `priorEntry is None` branch (same all-None BootReasonReport result); (d) recordBootReason gains a matching `sleeper` kwarg threaded through to detectBootReason; (e) `import time`; (f) module-header mod-history entry US-330. The US-308 graceful-detection body (_hasShutdownMarker / _probeLadderGraceful / SHUTDOWN_MARKERS / LADDER_GRACEFUL_* / the tail-probe + ladder-probe call sequence) is byte-untouched; writeStartupLog untouched (INSERT OR IGNORE idempotency preserved).)

tests/pi/diagnostics/test_boot_reason_v0276_graceful.py (NEW -- 7 tests / 4 classes; self-contained (_SequencedJournalctl stand-in with per-call --list-boots answers + listBootsCalls counter; _InMemoryStartupLogDb sticky-connection ObdDatabase shape; _NOSLEEP no-op sleeper). TestTransientListBootsFailureRecovered: V0.27.6-flavour slice ([None, _LIST_BOOTS_OUTPUT]) -> writer recovers prior_boot_clean=1 (FAILS pre-fix), detectBootReason retry-then-success ([None,None,output]) -> populated report + 3 --list-boots calls, happy-path -> exactly 1 --list-boots call. TestRetryExhausted: all-fail -> NULL row still written (not a crash) + LIST_BOOTS_RETRY_ATTEMPTS calls, all-fail emits a WARNING mentioning 'list-boots' (caplog). TestRetryDoesNotChangeClassification: crash boot after a --list-boots retry STILL classifies prior_boot_clean=0 (stop-condition #2 guard), recordBootReason retry-then-success is idempotent (one row).)

tests/pi/diagnostics/test_boot_reason.py (UPDATE -- 1-line: inject `sleeper=lambda _seconds: None` into test_detectBootReason_journalctlUnavailable_returnsReportWithNoneFields so the new US-330 --list-boots retry backoff doesn't make that legacy test block on real time.sleep (the post-retry result is identical to the pre-retry one -- all-None fields); + module-header mod-history entry US-330. No other behaviour change; this file's substantive coverage is unchanged.)

grounding.value: Pre-flight (code archaeology -- live `ssh chi-eclipse-01` is a CIO follow-up, same pattern as US-326 journalctl / US-327 ssh / US-328): writeStartupLog writes NULL for BOTH prior_boot_clean and prior_last_entry_ts only when report.priorBootClean is None AND report.priorLastEntryTs is None, i.e. detectBootReason's `priorEntry is None` branch, which is reached when `journalctl --list-boots` returns None (runJournalctl failure sentinel -- subprocess TimeoutExpired/FileNotFoundError/OSError). Hypothesis C (ladder shutdown sequence breaks the graceful regex) RULED OUT -- C would surface as prior_boot_clean=0 with prior_last_entry_ts POPULATED (the ts comes from --list-boots, not the tail); both-NULL means the failure is upstream of graceful detection.
grounding.source: Read of src/pi/diagnostics/boot_reason.py (writeStartupLog, detectBootReason) + the PM 2026-05-12 chi-eclipse-01 sqlite forensic in offices/pm/issues/I-030-startup-log-prior-boot-clean-regression-on-v0276.md

grounding.value: Hypothesis A (V0.27.6 US-322 orphan-cleanup.timer) is the LEADING trigger: it is the V0.27.5->V0.27.6 delta that fits the regression cliff -- orphan-cleanup.timer has Persistent=true (so it fires at boot to catch up the missed nightly 03:00 OnCalendar run) and orphan-cleanup.service runs `cleanup_orphan_realtime_data.py --execute` (a DELETE against data/obd.db on the Pi 5 SD card) concurrently with the launching eclipse-obd orchestrator; SD-card I/O starvation can push the orchestrator's early `journalctl --list-boots` subprocess past its 10s JOURNALCTL_TIMEOUT_SECONDS cap. The V0.27.6 deploy also `systemctl enable --now`'d the timer (deploy-pi.sh step_install_orphan_cleanup_unit), so it likely fired at the deploy AND at the next (post-Drain-17) boot. Hypothesis B (deploy changed boot order) is not supported by code -- the V0.27.6 deploy-pi.sh changes add no Before=/After= ordering that moves eclipse-obd.service earlier (the wifi.powersave drop-in could marginally hasten network.target -> a weak B-flavoured contributor at most).
grounding.source: git show 00b41af -- deploy/deploy-pi.sh + Read of deploy/orphan-cleanup.service|.timer + deploy/eclipse-obd.service + src/pi/obdii/orchestrator/lifecycle.py:_initializeAllComponents/_recordStartupLog + offices/pm/issues/I-030-startup-log-prior-boot-clean-regression-on-v0276.md hypotheses

grounding.value: Fix = the 'race-guard' path from US-330 scope (chosen over the unit-ordering path -- scope.filesToTouch is boot_reason.py + the test, and doNotTouch forbids touching orphan-cleanup.timer unless 'Hypothesis A confirmed AND fix requires unit reordering'; the race-guard breaks no invariants and is robust to A and any B-flavoured contributor): _readBootList retries `journalctl --list-boots` up to LIST_BOOTS_RETRY_ATTEMPTS=3 times with a LIST_BOOTS_RETRY_SLEEP_SECONDS=2.0 backoff (sleep DI'd through a `sleeper` kwarg on detectBootReason + recordBootReason); happy path returns on attempt 1 with zero backoff; a non-None-but-unparseable result is NOT retried (a genuine fresh Pi / pruned journal legitimately shows only the current boot); every-attempt-fails -> a WARNING ('journalctl --list-boots unavailable after N attempts ...') + the best-effort NULL row exactly as before (no silent failure -- the I-030 row was written silently; V0.24.1 anti-pattern lesson). The unit-ordering alternative (IOSchedulingClass=idle / After=eclipse-obd.service on orphan-cleanup.service) is filed as TD-051.

Real-world validation gate (story invariant + groundingRefs): Drain 18+ followed by a graceful boot on V0.27.7 -> the startup_log row has prior_boot_clean=1 + prior_last_entry_ts populated. The synthetic gate is tests/pi/diagnostics/test_boot_reason_v0276_graceful.py (7 tests, FAILS pre-fix). Residual CIO confirmation step (does NOT block the ship): `ls -la /var/log/journal/`, `journalctl --no-pager --list-boots | head -20`, `cat /etc/systemd/journald.conf.d/*`, `journalctl -u orphan-cleanup.service -n 30` on chi-eclipse-01 -- to confirm the trigger and rule out the two less-likely alternatives the symptom doesn't cleanly contradict (journald in volatile mode -> separate persistent-journald fix; a --list-boots output-format change from a Pi rebuild -> follow-up parseListBoots story). Both alternatives are harmless under this fix (a few seconds of bounded boot latency once + the new loud WARNING). See offices/pm/inbox/2026-05-12-from-rex-US-330-startup-log-fix-residual-live-confirm.md.

doNotTouch: V0.27.2 US-308 graceful detection function body (unless C confirmed)
startup_log table schema (US-263; correct)
orphan-cleanup.timer schedule (unless A confirmed AND reorder is the chosen fix)
stories.completionNotes: I-030 fix shipped via the 'race-guard' path (US-330 scope option). Pre-flight = code archaeology (live `ssh chi-eclipse-01` is a CIO follow-up, same established pattern as US-326 journalctl / US-327 ssh / US-328 this sprint): the PM forensic shows the V0.27.6 post-Drain-17 boot's startup_log row had BOTH prior_boot_clean AND prior_last_entry_ts empty -> in writeStartupLog that pair is NULL only when report.priorBootClean is None AND report.priorLastEntryTs is None -> in detectBootReason that is the `priorEntry is None` branch, reached when `journalctl --list-boots` returns None (the runJournalctl failure sentinel -- subprocess TimeoutExpired/FileNotFoundError/OSError; journalctl is on the service PATH so the likely sentinel is TimeoutExpired -- the 10s cap blown under boot-time I/O contention). HYPOTHESIS MAP: (C) ladder-regex-breaks -- RULED OUT (C would give prior_boot_clean=0 with prior_last_entry_ts POPULATED, since that ts comes from --list-boots not the tail; both-NULL means the failure is upstream of graceful detection). (A) V0.27.6 US-322 orphan-cleanup.timer -- LEADING: the V0.27.5->V0.27.6 delta that fits the regression cliff is the new orphan-cleanup.timer (Persistent=true -> fires at boot to catch up the missed 03:00 run) running cleanup_orphan_realtime_data.py --execute (a DELETE on data/obd.db on the SD card) concurrently with the launching orchestrator -> SD-card I/O starvation -> --list-boots times out -> None -> (pre-fix) immediate NULL row; the V0.27.6 deploy `enable --now`'d the timer so it likely fired at deploy AND next boot. (B) deploy changed boot order -- not supported by code (the V0.27.6 deploy-pi.sh adds no Before=/After= ordering moving eclipse-obd earlier; the wifi.powersave drop-in could let network.target be reached slightly sooner -- a weak B-flavoured contributor at most). FIX: detectBootReason now retries `journalctl --list-boots` via the new _readBootList helper (LIST_BOOTS_RETRY_ATTEMPTS=3, LIST_BOOTS_RETRY_SLEEP_SECONDS=2.0, sleep DI'd via a `sleeper` kwarg threaded through recordBootReason) so a transient storm doesn't strand the row; happy path returns on attempt 1 with zero backoff (the retry cost is only ever paid on the anomalous path); a non-None result is parsed as-is and NOT retried (a genuine fresh Pi / pruned journal legitimately shows only the current boot); when EVERY attempt fails it logs a WARNING (the I-030 row was written silently -- no silent failures, per the V0.24.1 anti-pattern lesson) and falls back to the best-effort NULL row exactly as before. The US-308 graceful-detection logic (tail-marker scan + V0.24.1 ladder -g probe) is BYTE-UNTOUCHED; doNotTouch honoured (no orphan-cleanup.timer change -- the in-scope race-guard was chosen over out-of-scope unit-reordering; the unit-ordering alternative [IOSchedulingClass=idle / After=eclipse-obd.service on orphan-cleanup.service] is filed as TD-051). Robust to A AND any B-flavoured contributor. NEW REGRESSION GATE tests/pi/diagnostics/test_boot_reason_v0276_graceful.py (7 tests / 4 classes): V0.27.6-flavour slice (--list-boots None on attempt 1, then real listing) -> writer recovers prior_boot_clean=1 (FAILS pre-fix -- pre-fix one --list-boots call + immediate give-up writes NULL); detectBootReason retry-then-success (3 calls) -> populated report; happy-path --list-boots called exactly once (no wasted retries/sleep); all-attempts-fail -> NULL row still written, not a crash, with COUNT==LIST_BOOTS_RETRY_ATTEMPTS calls; all-fail emits a WARNING mentioning 'list-boots' (caplog); crash boot after a --list-boots retry STILL classifies prior_boot_clean=0 (stop-condition #2 guard); recordBootReason retry-then-success is idempotent (INSERT OR IGNORE -- one row). Plus a 1-line no-op-`sleeper` injection into the existing test_detectBootReason_journalctlUnavailable_returnsReportWithNoneFields (the new retry now wraps that 'journalctl unavailable' path -- same all-None result, just don't block on real time.sleep). VERIFICATION all green: pytest tests/pi/diagnostics/test_boot_reason_v0276_graceful.py -v -> 7 PASS; pytest tests/pi/diagnostics/ -m 'not slow' -> 98 PASS (no regression on US-308/US-263 + drain forensics + Drive-11 forensic; no slow test introduced -- the affected legacy test is back to ~0s with the no-op sleeper); pytest tests/ -m 'not slow' --no-cov -> exit 0 (full fast suite GREEN; pre-change baseline also exit 0); ruff clean on src/pi/diagnostics/boot_reason.py + both test files (8 pre-existing ruff warnings in the untouched tests/pi/regression/test_powersource_module_identity.py are NOT mine -- out of scope, pre-existing tech debt); sprint_lint US-330 OK / 0 errors. CARRY for PM/CIO (see offices/pm/inbox/2026-05-12-from-rex-US-330-startup-log-fix-residual-live-confirm.md + TD-051): (1) live `ssh chi-eclipse-01` to CONFIRM the trigger -- `ls -la /var/log/journal/` (persistent storage present?), `journalctl --no-pager --list-boots | head -20` (does it now show prior boots, and in what format?), `cat /etc/systemd/journald.conf.d/*`, `journalctl -u orphan-cleanup.service -n 30` (ran at the post-Drain-17 boot? how long?). If /var/log/journal is missing or Storage=volatile, the real fix is restoring persistent journald (separate issue -- the step_install_journald_persistent deploy step exists but something defeated it); the retry doesn't fix that but does no harm + the new WARNING surfaces it. If --list-boots format is unparseable by parseListBoots (Pi rebuilt with newer systemd), that's a follow-up parseListBoots story. (2) Real-world validation gate (story invariant + groundingRefs): Drain 18+ followed by a graceful boot on V0.27.7 -> startup_log row has prior_boot_clean=1 + prior_last_entry_ts populated -- CIO drill check. (3) acceptance #4 backfill of the empty Drain-17-boot row -- PM's call; one-row UPDATE keyed on that boot_id (clean V0.24.1 ladder shutdown -> prior_boot_clean=1); low value, I'd skip it. (4) Still open from earlier sprints: BL-014 (harness .claude/commands write gate from US-318).
